original_prompt:
Is this tweet relevant to on-chain AI agents, agent trading, DeFi automation, or the agent economy?

Tweet: 🚨BREAKING: Anthropic just dropped something that should terrify every hacker on the planet.

They built an AI called Claude Mythos that found thousands of hidden security flaws in every major operating system and every major web browser.

One bug had been hiding undetected for 27 years.

And it found them all on its own. No human guidance. Fully autonomous.

This is Project Glasswing. And it's the most important AI announcement nobody is talking about today.

Here's what actually happened.

Anthropic realized their new model had become so good at reading code that it could find security holes better than almost any human alive. Instead of keeping it quiet, they called up AWS, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Cisco, NVIDIA, CrowdStrike, and seven other tech giants.

Together they're using Mythos to scan and fix the software that runs your bank, your hospital, your power grid, and your government.

Anthropic committed $100M in free usage credits to make it happen.

The 27-year-old bug it found in OpenBSD. A 16-year-old flaw in FFmpeg that automated tools had tested five million times without catching. A chain of Linux kernel vulnerabilities that gave attackers full machine control.

All found. All patched. All gone.

The scary part isn't that AI can find these bugs. The scary part is that bad actors will have this same capability soon.

Project Glasswing is the race to fix everything before that happens.

https://t.co/GIURLuwoxT

What do you think?

Reply with ONLY "relevant" or "irrelevant".
